pregnancyCalories.name: Pregnancy Calorie Needs
pregnancyCalories.description: Calculate your daily calorie needs by trimester.

meta.title: Pregnancy Calorie Needs Calculator — kcal by Trimester
meta.description: Calculate your pregnancy calorie needs by trimester using the IOM EER formula. Pre-pregnancy weight, height, age and activity. Twin pregnancy supported. Free, instant.

pregnancyCalories.title: Pregnancy Calorie Needs
pregnancyCalories.description: Estimate your daily calorie requirements during each trimester — based on IOM recommendations.
pregnancyCalories.twinsHint: Adds +300 kcal/day in T2 and T3
pregnancyCalories.implausibleAge: Please enter an age between 18 and 50 years.
pregnancyCalories.implausibleWeight: Please enter a pre-pregnancy weight between 35 and 200 kg (77 and 440 lb).
pregnancyCalories.implausibleHeight: Please enter a height between 130 and 210 cm (51 and 83 in).
pregnancyCalories.howItWorksText: The calculator first estimates your pre-pregnancy need with the IOM EER formula: EER = 354 − 6.91·age + PA·(9.36·weight + 726·height). It then adds the pregnancy supplement: 0 kcal in T1, +340 kcal in T2, +452 kcal in T3. For twin pregnancies, an additional +300 kcal/day is added in T2 and T3.
pregnancyCalories.disclaimer: This is a population-based estimate. Individual needs vary with weight gain, metabolism, multiples or comorbidities. Always consult your midwife or doctor.

faq.q: How many extra calories do I need during pregnancy?
faq.a: Per IOM: 0 kcal in the 1st trimester, +340 kcal/day in the 2nd, and +452 kcal/day in the 3rd. Twin pregnancies add another +300 kcal/day starting in T2.

faq.q: Do I really need no extra calories in T1?
faq.a: Energetically, the increase in the first trimester is essentially zero. What matters more is nutrient density — folate, iodine, iron and overall food quality.

faq.q: What does 'eating for two' really mean?
faq.a: It's a myth. The extra need maxes out at about one snack (300–450 kcal). Food quality matters more than quantity.

faq.q: Which formula does the calculator use?
faq.a: The IOM EER formula for adult women (DRI 2002/2005), combined with the trimester-specific energy supplement.
Active means 3–5 sessions per week of moderate activity (brisk walking, swimming, yoga). 'Very active' covers competitive sport — rarely recommended during pregnancy.

faq.q: Should I lose weight if I start pregnancy overweight?
faq.a: Active weight loss is not recommended. Aim for the lower end of the IOM weight-gain ranges (5–9 kg in obesity). Always coordinate with your doctor or midwife.
